observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management assess and review its Organizational Structure and establish a Staffing Pattern Plan and written policy or manual on hiring of Contract of Services/Job-Order.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has a CSC- approved Strategic Performance Management System which implements the performance management for government workers. As such, during the Performance Target Setting and Evaluation the Department Heads present the achievement or the failure to dos so, of their Major Final Outputs. Central to this discussion is the manpower requirement of each department and the submission of corresponding requests for additional manpower. At the end of each performance rating period, the DH recommends any of the following – the hiring of additional manpower, transfer of JOs or in worst case, the non-renewal of their contracts. Thus, it can be said that the hiring Job Orders is a result of performance management system. As to Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern, JOs are not included in the official structure there being no employer-employee relationship. But for internal control purposes, each Department submits Org Structure with the JOs to the MHRMDO for monitoring and evaluation purposes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer have to process immediately the claims for remittances to GSIS to avoid the imposition of penalties. observations.managementAction:
Preparation of salary and corresponding remittances for newly-promoted and newly-appointed employees is contingent upon their submission of complete required attachments for appointment to the position. 
Specifically, the duly received copies of the submitted Appointment documents by CSC Surigao Field Office and approved DTRs shall be attached to the DVs. Claim of Maternity Leave Benefits is contingent upon the employee and the submission of complete required documents/attachments. Employees who submitted their DTRs and attachments after the cut-off period resulted to late processing of their salaries and remittances. 
Regular and Casual payrolls were/are submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management set timelines for the Offices of the Human Resource Management, Accounting Office and Treasury to expedite the remittances for PhilHealth and remit the due amount to the latter on or before the tenth (10th) calendar day of the month following the applicable month for which the payment is due and applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant maintain Property Cards and Ledger Cards, respectively. Further, direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the Reports on the Physical Count on all PPEs of the LGU and to disclose the total Road Network System in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management ensure that cash advances of the concerned accountable officers be liquidated in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

Further, exhaust all possible remedies and demand immediate liquidation of the foregoing unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Assessor exert utmost efforts to reconcile the balances of Land Account. Likewise, record the unrecorded TDs in the SLs to substantiate the propriety and reasonableness of the said account.

We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and other concerned personnel exert all efforts to register all land owned by the Municipality under the Torrens Title System in conformity with COA Circular No. 92-386.

Further, reconciliation between the records of the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Assessor should be regularly made to accurately establish the valuation of land in the books of accounts of the Municipality.

Furthermore, inspection of the unrecorded land with the presence of the Auditor be made to verify the existence and current condition of the same.

We recommended that the Municipality cancel immediately the deed of donation. Any construction or improvement made by CLAMECCO on the said lot shall be demolished or removed not at the expense of the former but by the latter.

We recommended that the Management submit religiously the list of on-going and to be implemented PPAs for calendar year 2020 and at the beginning of the year thereafter, and inform COA Audit Team within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure projects or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices are already posted in conformity with the requirements of Sections 2.1 and 3.1 of the aforementioned Circular.

We recommended the refund of the financial assistance given to CLAMECCO.

Likewise, prohibit disbursing funds to Programs, Activities and Projects in the absence of an appropriation for the same.

We recommended that the Management comply with Section 53(e) of R.A. 9184 and Section 4 of Administrative Order No. 17 as well as Paragraph 1.1 of DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 dated August 25, 2011 in the procurement of Common-Use Supplies from PS-DBM.

We recommended that the BAC, in its next purchases, ensure that the bidder who has the lowest calculated responsive bid should be the one to be awarded.

Likewise, submit a written explanation as to why the procurement was awarded to the second lowest calculated responsive bidder.

We recommended that Management:
Submit a written explanation why the foregoing violation was committed; and
Religiously adhere to Section 2 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 on the procurement activities covered by the provision of the said RA.

We recommended that Management:
Submit the legal basis for awarding the contract to supplier considering that they were ineligible due to the absence of eligibility documents; and,
Direct the BAC to be more cautious in evaluating the legal, technical and financial documents submitted by bidders during preliminary evaluation and post-qualification before recommending the award of the contract.

We recommended that Management require the BAC and Supply Officer to submit to the Auditor’s Office the certified copies of the duly signed contracts and purchase orders, respectively, within five working days upon its perfection in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 to timely review and verify the transactions and immediately validate the compliance with the terms and conditions stipulated therein.

We recommended that Management require the Inspectorate Team/Committee to furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of the IARs within twenty- four (24) hours after the acceptance of all consumable deliveries and/or soon after the inspection and acceptance by the personnel concerned on other items so that timely inspection thereof can be conducted by the Auditors.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the concerned offices charging repairs and maintenance to completely attach the documentary requirements required under Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before approving the same.

Further, submission of the lacking documentary requirements is hereby required.

Furthermore, the pre-repair and post-repair inspections should have to be done by the person who has technical knowledge and expertise in repairing motor vehicles.

We recommended that the Management stop the giving of rewards to any person on drug-related arrest without first securing the recommendation from the Dangerous Drugs Board in conformity with Section 22 of RA No. 9165.

We recommended that the Management avoid payments of the last and final billing of infrastructure projects without requiring first the last two documentary requirements of Section 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Likewise, submission of the lacking documentary requirements of the final payments contained in Annex L is hereby required.
We recommended that the Management hire/employ an Internal Auditor to ensure the quality of performance and compliance with laws, rules and regulations as mandated in AO No. 70.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor give fully to the MSWDO the responsibility of granting financial assistance to those entitled in view of the well-settled principle that the general welfare clause in the Local Government Code does not imply a dole out of government funds and to the fundamental principle that government funds should be spent or used solely for public purposes.

Further, we recommended that the Head of the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office create data bank of information of all indigents per barangay for ready reference in processing financial assistance and other related transactions. Together with the Department Heads concerned, study the flow of the transaction documents from inception, processing, authorization and payment of financial assistance. From thereon, establish and set up clear-cut policies and guidelines on its program of granting donations, financial and other form of assistance. The beneficiaries, amounts and documentary requirements and the processing office/s should be well-defined to ensure that only qualified beneficiaries are assisted and that assistance is given only for public and general welfare purpose.

We recommended that Management:
Prepare and submit the monthly report on fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format of the foregoing LBC;
Post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality at the end of every month; and
Send a written notice when said reports have been posted on its website to above mentioned government agencies.

We recommended that the Management prepare and submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances with supporting documents to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (NDRRMC), copy furnished the Audit Team for all donations received, COVID-19 or non-COVID-19 related supplies and equipment from the private companies in calendar year 2020 and every quarter thereafter, if there is any.
We recommended that the Management strictly utilize the BGCM funds for purposes enumerated under Section 3.5 of LBC No. 125.

We recommend that Management refrain from utilizing the LDRRMF for non-disaster related programs, projects and/or activities in conformity with Section 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 in order to attain the purpose for which the fund was intended.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue an Executive Order or Administrative Order to create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team and direct the GAD Focal Point System to prepare the necessary status and evaluation reports for submission to DILG.

We recommended that Management stop appropriating and disbursing funds from the share in the National Wealth Fund which could not be construed as local development and livelihood projects as provided for under Section 294 of RA 7160.

The Municipal Accountant disclose the total road network system in the Notes to the Financial Statements in consonance with Section VI.2 of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommended Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the recorded Asset accounts to appropriate CIP – Agency Asset Accounts and to timely record all projects based on percentage of completion as inspected by the LGU.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the                          Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to recognize liability only when the obligation is founded on a valid claim that is properly supported by sufficient evidence and there is proper authority for its incurrence. Require the Municipal Accountant to substantiate the Accounts Payable account to ensure the fair presentation of the financial statements and to settle the obligation within 12 months after the reporting date.

We recommended Management to require the Municipal Accountant to immediately demand the liquidation of funds transferred and submission of supporting documents on the utilization of the fund received by the LGUs concerned. Otherwise, require the LGUs for the settlement/ return of the fund if not used to its intended purpose. Likewise, incorporate in the Memorandum Agreement the responsibilities of the implementing agency set forth in               COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management  hire additional manpower for the Municipal Accountant’s Office to facilitate the maintenance of subsidiary ledgers and the Municipal Accountant to submit the same with the trial balance and financial statements for all special accounts of the LGU as part of the schedules of the General Fund in adherence of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. Likewise, direct the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate bank account for the existing economic enterprise of the LGU through Sanggunian Bayan resolution.

We recommended that Management direct the LEEDMO In-Charge to furnish the Municipal Accountant’s Office of the billing statements of all leased premises and for the Municipal Accountant thereon to fully account and record the rental fees in the books of the LGU.

We recommended Management to direct the End-User not to accept the goods delivered and the Inspectorate Team not to sign the Acceptance and Inspection Report when no Purchase Order/Contract was issued. Further, require the Bids and Award Committee to ensure that execution of Purchase Order/ Contract to be done prior to the delivery of goods procured. Likewise, direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to review the supporting documents before processing the claims.

We recommended that Management adhere strictly the provisions of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in appropriating and utilizing 20% of the Annual Internal Revenue Allotment for development projects.

We recommended Management to appropriate properly the 70% of the LDRRMF in accordance with the programs and projects enumerated in Sections 5.1 to 5.4 NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and optimally utilize the fund to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks. Likewise, furnish the Auditor copies of SL of all accounts for SEF and TF to facilitate audit of said accounts.

We recommended that Management stop appropriating the 20% DF for repair and Maintenance of Garbage Truck/ Ambulance/Dump Truck and utilizing the fund to rental of heavy equipment, repair of mini dump truck and retention for the repair and maintenance of street lights  and others in adherence to DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 for more priority development projects.

We recommended that Management utilize the LDRRMF solely to QRF and disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness PPAs. Otherwise, charge the expenses to General Fund. Moreover, direct:



1. The MDRRM Council to decide on the use of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF, and review and update annually the LDRRMIFIP to incorporate the PPAs to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund; and

2. The Municipal Accountant’s Office, MDRRM Office, MDRRM Council and Municipal Development Council to prepare and submit the monthly and annual Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the auditor using the format in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive strictly comply with the mandate of RA 9003, known as the Ecological and Solid waste Management Act of 2000, especially on the establishment of solid waste and materials recovery facilities and closure of the open dumpsite as provided in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2009-168 dated October 27, 2009. Moreover, stop incurrence of expenditures not in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that Management:

1. Require the Local School Board to ensure that identified PPAs are implemented within the timeframe to give schools, teachers and students better facilities which are necessary in providing quality education; and to include only expenditure items provided under Section 4.0 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and those contained in the approved School Improvement Plan (SIP) and the Division Education Development Plan (DEDP) duly supported with documentary requirements;

2. Stop using the Special Education Fund for donations to other municipalities and barangays outside LGU Claver as these are not among those mentioned in Sections 1.0 and 4.0 of said Circular; and

3. Stop giving the SEF to individuals asking for assistance /donations. Instead transfer the needed funds to the DepEd Surigao del Norte Division to avoid possible loss and/ or misuse of government funds.

We recommended that Management direct the members of the LCPC and Municipal Social Welfare Development Office:

1. To closely monitor and implement the PPAs for LCPC and to maximize the utilization of the LCPC fund. 

2.  To prepare Children Profile and to establish data base on children in order to properly plan and identify the PPAs which is the most need of the children in the municipality for full delivery of the services as enumerated in Section II of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant’s Office to submit the RCI with the DVs, and RCDs with the official receipts and validated deposit slips within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month as provided in Section 7.2.1.a of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that:
1. The Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF and                    fast-track the implementation of the unimplemented and on-going development projects as of December 31, 2017; and

2. The Municipal Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to closely monitor and strongly pursue the timely and full implementation of all the programs and projects of the 20% DF and to regularly submit status reports to the Municipal Mayor so that appropriate measures can be instituted.

We recommended that Management optimally use the 70% allocation of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund through the implementation of the projects and activities allowed and mentioned in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks.

We recommended that Management direct the GAD Focal Point System to request for training from Philippine Commission on Women or any authorized government agencies to increase their knowledge and build their competencies on GAD related laws, gender analysis and assessment. Submit immediately the GAD Accomplishment Report and comply Section V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 on the prescribed period in the submission of GAD AR to COA Audit Team.

We recommend that the accountable officers concerned strictly comply with the regulations on the liquidation of cash advances as mentioned in Section 1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.  Otherwise, failure of the AO to liquidate his cash advance within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause for the withholding of his salary as provided for in Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and the instruction of other sanctions as provided for under paragraph 9.2 and 9.3 thereof. Likewise, failure to liquidate the cash advances in aggregate amounts of Fifty Thousand Pesos (₱50,000.00) and above per accountable officer within the period prescribed shall constitute administrative offense of Gross Neglect of Duty or Simple Neglect of Duty, as the case may be, pursuant to CSC Resolution No. 1200103 dated January 12, 2012. Further, we also recommend the Municipal Accountant to include all cash advances of special disbursing officers, disbursing officers and officials and employees in the Monthly and Quarterly 

Monitoring Report of Cash Advances. Fill up properly and completely the data needed in the said Report to provide the public with accurate information.

observations.recommendation: 1.7		We recommended Management to require the Municipal Accountant                  to record only valid reconciling items in the GL, and to prepare BRS on                     all bank accounts within ten (10) days from receipt of the bank statements                   to ensure the fair presentation of the financial statements.

2.8	We recommended Management to require the General Service Officer (GSO) and/ or the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count on all PPE                     owned by the LGU and prepare report thereon to be submitted not later than January 31 of each year. Likewise, direct the GSO and the Municipal Accountant                 to keep and maintain Property Cards and Local Road Network Property Cards; and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card, Real Property Ledger Card, Construction in Progress Ledger Card, Registry of Public Infrastructures and Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively.

3.7		We recommended Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the recorded Asset accounts to appropriate CIP – Agency Asset Accounts and to timely record all projects based on percentage of completion as inspected by the LGU.

4.9		We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the                          Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to recognize liability only when the obligation is founded on a valid claim that is properly supported by sufficient evidence and there is proper authority for its incurrence.                     Require the Municipal Accountant to substantiate the Accounts Payable account to ensure the fair presentation of the financial statements and to settle the obligation within 12 months after the reporting date.

5.6	We recommended Management to require the Municipal Accountant                    to immediately demand the liquidation of funds transferred and submission of supporting documents on the utilization of the fund received by the LGUs concerned. Otherwise, require the LGUs for the settlement/ return of the fund if not used to its intended purpose. Likewise, incorporate in the Memorandum Agreement the responsibilities of the implementing agency set forth in               COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

6.8	We recommended Management to monitor and inspect the project implementation of the recipient NGOs/POs based on the provision of the MOA and approved project proposal and to demand the liquidation of funds transferred by complying the documentary requirements enumerated in                 Section 2.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. Otherwise, require the NGOs/POs for the settlement/ return of the fund if not used to its intended purpose. Further, direct the Municipal Accountant to provide the details of P2,538,877.88  in compliance with Section 5.2 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007.

7.8	We recommended that Management  hire additional manpower for the Municipal Accountant’s Office to facilitate the maintenance of subsidiary ledgers and the Municipal Accountant to submit the same with the trial balance and financial statements for all special accounts of the LGU as part of the schedules of the General Fund in adherence of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. Likewise, direct the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate bank account for the existing economic enterprise of the LGU through Sanggunian Bayan resolution.

8.8	We recommended that Management direct the LEEDMO In-Charge to furnish the Municipal Accountant’s Office of the billing statements of all leased premises and for the Municipal Accountant thereon to fully account and record the rental fees in the books of the LGU.

9.8		We recommended Management to direct the End-User not to accept the goods delivered and the Inspectorate Team not to sign the Acceptance and Inspection Report when no Purchase Order/Contract was issued. Further, require the Bids and Award Committee to ensure that execution of Purchase Order/ Contract to be done prior to the delivery of goods procured. Likewise, direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to review the supporting documents before processing the claims.

10.7		We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to remit promptly the unexpended balance on completed projects to the BTr. Further, direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary journal entries on the return of the amount to close the                Due to NGAs account in adherence with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-3,                 DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2018-61 and COA Circular No. 94-013.

11.4		We recommended that Management adhere strictly the provisions of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in appropriating and utilizing 20% of the Annual Internal Revenue Allotment for development projects.

12.12	We recommended Management to appropriate properly the 70% of the LDRRMF in accordance with the programs and projects enumerated in           Sections 5.1 to 5.4 NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and optimally utilize the fund to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks. Further, charging of traveling expenses, as one of the maintenance and other operating expenses, shall be in the General Fund and not to the 70% of the LDRRMF.

13.6		We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Solid Waste Board to prepare and submit an annual progress report on the strategies implemented and accomplishments to the National Solid Waste Management through its Secretariat in compliance with NSWMC Resolution No. 714                 series   of 2016.

14.8	We recommended that Management direct the Requisitioner/End-User not to accept the goods delivered and the Inspectorate Team not to sign the Acceptance and Inspection Report if Purchase Order/Contract was not issued. Further, require the Bids and Award Committee to  ensure that execution of Purchase Order/Contract be done prior to the delivery of goods procured and not to resort to splitting just to evade competitive bidding. Likewise,                          direct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to require from persons concerned the lacking supporting documents before claims are processed.

15.5	We recommended Management to require the accountable officers concerned to fast track the processing of the remittance of Provincial share from the additional one percent (1%) tax on real property accruing to the SEF to meet the payment thereof with fifteen (15) days of every succeeding month in compliance with Section 9 of RA 5447.

16.4	We recommended that Management direct the LEEDMO In-Charge and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to strictly enforce the Lease Contracts and the provisions in Sections 67 to 69 of Municipal Ordinance No. 2017-32 known as the Revenue Code of 2017 of the Municipality of Claver, Province of Surigao                 del Norte. Likewise, the Management has to install systems from the numbering of stalls, application, billing, collection and accounting of the rental fees in coordination with the LEEDMO, MTO and Municipal Accountant’s Office.

17.7	We recommend Management to instruct the LEEDMO In-charge to prepare a  5-Year Business Plan for each LEE in compliance with Section 5.3 of the Manual on the Setting Up and Operation of the Local Economic Enterprises (2016) and be guided of the provisions therein.

18.7		We recommended that Management direct the GAD Focal Point System to properly plan and regularly monitor the implementation of                      GAD PPAs, to ensure its full utilization and to help promote empowerment of women, pursue equal opportunities for women and men, and to ensure equal access to resources and to development results and outcome. Moreover,                             if there is a need to change and/or implement additional PPAs as a result of regular monitoring, the Local Chief Executive shall inform the concerned                 DILG Office in writing in accordance with Section 6.0 of PCW-DILG- DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016.
We recommended that Management require the concerned persons liable to settle their disallowances in accordance with the Revised RRSA.

We recommend that the Management require the:
1. Acting Municipal Accountant to reconcile the entries in the Cashbook and Subsidiary/General Ledger (SL/GL) of the previous accountable officers to avoid accumulation of variance an submit the adjusting entries on the reconciled amounts on collections and deposits for calendar year 2017.

2. Municipal Accountants Office to submit immediately all the lacking SL for the Cash-Local Treasury account as of December 31, 2017 and to prepare and submit the JEVs for every RCD of the Municipal Treasurer for calendar year 2017 as required in Section 35(e and f) of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume 1.

3. Revenue Collectors to turn over the collections daily to the Liquidating Officer /Municipal Treasurer and the latter to deposit immediately her undeposited collections as of December 31, 2017 to avoid possible loss of government funds and the LGU could use the fund for public purpose.

We recommended that Management: 
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to record immediately the book reconciling items/ discrepancies amounting to P64, 190,154.37 in the respective general ledgers for air presentation of the Cash in Bank account balance and other affected accounts;

2.  Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payees of the stale checks totaling P38,743.00 and strictly observe Section 59 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I on the procedures for reporting thereof. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to draw journal entry voucher for proper recording and accounting of stale checks and cancelled ones; and

3. Instruct the Municipal Accountant’s Office to prepare and submit correct Monthly BRS on all accounts pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445 and immediately submit the SLs on four (4) bank accounts.

We recommend that Local Chief Executive require the:
1. Bids and Awards Committee with coordination of the Municipal Engineer to submit at the beginning of the year to the Auditor a list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those that are to be implemented during the year as provided for in Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2014. Likewise, maintain and submit a Monthly Monitoring Report on the Project Status every 3rd day of the following month.

2. Contractor/Supplier to post a tarpaulin signboard at the project location as soon as the award has been made using the design and format as shown in Annex “A” of the same Circular. We recommend that the:
1. Bids and Awards Committee require the winning bidder to post performance security within the period as stipulated in the Notice of Award and prior to the signing of the contract in adherence to Section 39.1 of the Revised IIR of RA 9184;

2. Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) award the contract only if the winning bidder post a performance security prior to the signing of the contract and forfeit the bid securities for failure to post pursuant to Section 41 (b) of Revised IRR of RA 9184; and Section 40.1 of Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and Section 5 of GPPB issued Resolution No. 15-2014 dated 30 August 2013; and

3. HOPE enforce the Bid Securing Declaration of the winning bidder and demand in writing the payment of the fine within fifteen (15) days from receipt of the written demand as provided for in GPPB Resolution                   No. 15-2014 dated 30 August 2013.

We recommend that management :
1. Direct the BAC Secretariat to submit the contracts/purchase orders and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5 working days from its execution to the Auditor pursuant to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. Otherwise, any unjustified failure of the officials and employees concerned to comply with the requirements herein imposed shall be subject to the administrative disciplinary action provided in (a) Section 127 of Presidential Decree No. 1445; (b) Section 55, Title I-B, Book V of the Revised Administrative Code of 1987; and (c) Section 11 of Republic Act No. 6713.




2. Require Engineering Office to monitor and evaluate Contractors compliance with the contract and its obligations to commence works on the start date as provided for in the Notice to Proceed.

Further, we recommend the Management to submit the MOA/TA to the Auditor within five (5) working days from the execution of a contract in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

The same shall be done on each ensuing semesters and years thereafter.

We recommend that Management compel the Property Officer, Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to adhere the provisions of Custodianship and Issuance and Utilization in the Training Handbook on Property and Supply Management System. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality for inventory-          

taking is an indispensable procedure for checking the integrity of property custodianship. Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) upon completion of the physical count and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year. Reconcile the said report with the accounting and property records. Moreover, instruct the Property Officer to maintain property cards and renew ARE every January after the third (3rd) year of issue; and the Municipal Accountant to maintain Construction in Progress Ledger Card (CIPLC), Real Property Ledger Card (RPLC), Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC). Require all accountable officers issued with equipment to return the unserviceable property to the Property Officer and let the latter prepare the IIRUP for the Inspection Officer to validate such condition with the presence of the Auditor concerned.

We recommend the Management to:

Compel and require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers and prepare trial balance and   financial statements for all special accounts of the LGU to form part as schedules of the General Fund as provided for in Sections 105, 107 and 110 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and to serve as an effective

 tool in strategy formulation and decision-making activities of the concerned offices; and

Pass a resolution through the Sanggunian Bayan authorizing the LGU for the opening of separate bank accounts for Market Operations, Transportation System and Waterworks System.

We recommend that Management direct the Solid Waste Management Board to require the Barangays Hayanggabon, Bagakay and Taganito to allocate a certain parcel of land through its Sanggunian for the establishment of MRF. Direct all Punong Barangays to comply the guidelines and criteria set in Section 33 of RA 9003 in the establishment of MRF to accommodate efficient and safe materials processing, movement, and storage; and to allow efficient and safe external access and to accommodate internal flow. We recommend that Local Chief Executive to close the open dump site, have a sanitary landfill site in accordance with Sections 41 and 42 to protect personnel and public health and safety; and establish and operate the Eco-Solid Waste Management Park based on its description and purpose provided for in NSWMC Resolution No. 79, Series of 2014.

Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the Reports to the Auditor of the LGU and to the Source Agency (SA) (Attention: The SA Accountant) within ten (10) days after receipt from the AO as mentioned in COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994. Further, we recommend the Management to submit the MOA/TA to the Auditor within five (5) working days from the execution of a contract in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

21.4	We recommend that the GAD Focal Point System establish and maintain GAD Database to have adequate information for gender-responsiveness, programming and policy making using Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
We recommended that Management require the concerned persons liable to settle their disallowances in accordance with the Revised RRSA.
